objective: Identify gaps between traditional linters/static analysis tools and the MetaBlooms Meta-Linter (MBML).

gaps.description: No intent awareness in traditional linters
gaps.impact: False positives and inability to judge correctness relative to goals
gaps.mbml_resolution: Attach intent/context rows and score violations against stated goals

gaps.description: No cross-artifact reasoning (code vs docs vs chats)
gaps.mbml_resolution: Unified row schema across all artifacts enabling cross-diffing

gaps.impact: Noise overwhelms signal; teams ignore lint output
gaps.mbml_resolution: Historical false-positive suppression and quality-weighted scoring

gaps.description: Lack of behavioral and execution-posture analysis
gaps.impact: Cannot detect fake execution, silent downgrade, or policy theater
gaps.mbml_resolution: Behavioral linting using execution-mode and artifact emission checks

gaps.description: No regression protection against quality decay
gaps.impact: Slow erosion of system quality despite passing checks
gaps.mbml_resolution: Quality Episode baselines and MQS regression gates

gaps.impact: Multiple tools needed with no unifying model
gaps.mbml_resolution: Meta-layer orchestration that treats linters as sensors

non_goals: Replacing language-level syntax or compiler checks
Reimplementing AST parsing for each language
constraints: MBML must defer to best-in-class linters for syntax correctness
MBML must remain non-blocking except for safety or critical regression
